_comment: Adds the RHBK 26.4 cluster as a trusted OpenID Connect Identity Provider
and creates a dedicated client for the Token Exchange flow.
IMPORTANT: Before importing, enable the Token Exchange tech-preview feature
on all RH-SSO nodes by adding to standalone.xml (or standalone-ha.xml):
Replace ALL placeholders marked with < > before importing.

clients._comment: Dedicated client for the Token Exchange Proxy to perform exchanges
clients.name: Token Exchange Service Client
clients.description: Used by the Token Exchange Proxy to swap RHBK tokens for native RH-SSO tokens

_setup_notes.step_1: Import this file via Admin Console -> Realm Settings -> Partial Import, or use kcadm.sh
_setup_notes.step_2: After import, go to the 'token-exchange-client' -> Permissions tab
_setup_notes.step_4: Click on 'token-exchange' permission -> add a policy that allows the 'rhbk' identity provider
_setup_notes.step_5: Verify: the Token Exchange Proxy should now be able to POST to the token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange
